synthesis.thesis: An attractive architecture gathered momentum, was challenged against the real requirements, then superseded without losing the learning it produced.
synthesis.story: The governance situation is not a simple mistake. The vault-centered plan had real attractions: editability, local durability, and familiar human workflows. The harder judgment was recognising when those attractions started to overdetermine the substrate. The salvage thread turns the previous work into evidence for a corrected basis instead of pretending the detour did not happen.
synthesis.orgExperience: The evidence shows momentum with an escape hatch. A promising direction can be explored, learned from, stopped, and converted into a cleaner decision basis before sunk cost becomes governance.
synthesis.mechanism: Supersession links preserve the abandoned vault-centered path while moving the active decision basis to a substrate derived from CML requirements.
synthesis.demonstrates: This is sunk-cost resistance made visible. The record shows a willingness to abandon a plausible path after it has accumulated work, while preserving enough evidence for future actors to understand why the path changed.

intent.description: Move LabRuntime agent coordination architecture to a dedicated Obsidian vault as the shared persistent state and human inspection layer, replacing the custom LabRuntime runtime build. The vault substrate — CouchDB (LiveSync) + typed markdown frontmatter + Obsidian UI — provides distributed state, revision tracking, human inspectability, and a plugin ecosystem for transformation without the maintenance cost of a custom runtime. The coordination layer (Lab Bridge) shifts from context mediator to transport/orchestration, with the vault as the persistent shared state agents read from and write to. This vault is completely separate from Cleanroom, which remains a personal metacognition cleanroom.
intent.resolutionNotes: Superseded by CML-30. See interpretation filed by code-framework on CML-30 for the diagnosis, the R1–R11 substrate-requirement derivation, the candidate evaluation (Git as leading; CouchDB+LiveSync fails R11/R6), and the proposed salvage shape. Salvageable artifacts (CleanroomCode CouchDB feasibility findings, Cowork ergonomics inventory) to be preserved as Reports against CML-30. Vault-schema-v2 paused indefinitely; v1 not to be refined. Misfiled architecture sketches in Cleanroom/workspace/architecture/ to be relocated. CLI-first compute-economy goal preserved but decoupled — implementable as LabRuntime + SqliteBridgeRepository + CLI binary against existing Bridge SQLite.

recentEvents.reason: Salvage brainstorm 2026-04-15: CML-26 collapsed three concerns (LabRuntime persistence, CML read/write surface, Cleanroom-parallel substrate) into one architecture (Obsidian on CouchDB+LiveSync). The collapse propagated cyclical inference-by-toolchain into LabRuntime's contract surface (VaultGovernanceRepository pulling CouchDB _rev / validate_doc_update semantics) and forced governance enforcement into a storage validator. Superseded by CML-30, which re-derives substrate requirements from the v0.1/v0.2 CML schemas before any substrate commitment.

intent.description: Vault schema spec v1 — frontmatter contracts for the coordination vault (cml-vault CouchDB database). Sub-intent of CML-26. Deliverable: a single spec document that defines frontmatter for all 8 entity types (intent, interpretation, action, claim, expertise-signal, event, actor, domain), the state machines, the invariants that must hold on any backend, and a directory layout. Purpose: unblocks CleanroomCode's validate_doc_update design doc + bootstrap-couchdb-cml.sh, and unblocks LabCode's VaultGovernanceRepository TypeScript mapping. Review cycle: CleanroomCode (infra feasibility, CouchDB specifics) → Cowork (Dataview/Templater/human-editing ergonomics) → Seb (governance vocabulary sign-off) → v2 accepted → bootstrap runs. Seed artifact at mirror/vault-schema-v1.md.

intent.description: Supersedes CML-26. Pursue the next CML primitives layer by deriving substrate requirements from the v0.1/v0.2 CML schemas, then choosing a substrate against that requirement list. Out of scope until requirements are validated: any commitment to Obsidian / CouchDB / LiveSync as the substrate, and any change to LabRuntime's GovernanceRepository contract surface to accommodate a specific backend. In scope: (1) specify the missing v0.1 primitives the Bridge does not yet model — Conflict Expression and Authority Surface, with associated diagnostic states (Divergence Count, Resolution Lag, Interpretation Density, Version Pressure); (2) derive an explicit substrate-requirement list from v0.1/v0.2 and validate it with Seb; (3) evaluate substrate candidates (filesystem-markdown, git-as-substrate, event-log+projection, CouchDB+LiveSync) against that list before committing to one. The compute-economy goal of replacing the MCP Bridge with a CLI-first surface is preserved but decoupled from the substrate decision and may proceed in parallel via LabRuntime + a SqliteBridgeRepository reading the existing Bridge SQLite directly, no vault required for that step.

actions.description: Filed INT-87 as the aligned position on CML-30, superseding INT-85. Synthesizes INT-86 (cowork-theory sharpening of R10 / shared-cognition / mediation-vs-ledger) and Seb's Q1–Q4 answers: R1–R14 as final derived requirement set with R10 split (a/b), R12 active-awareness added, R13 retrospective reinterpretation added, R14 expertise matching added; real-time latency explicitly rejected; Conflict Expression + Authority Surface deferred; lab grows into mediation surface (R10b binds); single substrate (Q4). Substrate recommendation shifted from Git to purpose-built — keeping ≈5% of Git (content-addressable append-only log, multi-device replication, attribution, legible projection, linear history), adding native push/notify (the 1 thing), dropping ≈95% (branches, merges, rebase, force-push, refs namespace, index, submodules, etc.). R11 preserved via Git-compatible read projection. Path A confirmed with target = purpose-built. CML-30 split into 30.1 (CLI-first compute-economy via SqliteBridgeRepository), 30.2 (substrate spec + build, likely further split 30.2a/30.2b), 30.3 (dispatcher — matcher + 80/20 autonomy, with agent-backlog as tie-break for equal-capability matches). INT-87 alignment bumped to `aligned` per Seb directive. Mirror written to mirror/cml-30-aligned-position-2026-04-15.md per R9 discipline.
actions.outcome: success — INT-85 superseded by INT-87 (aligned/proposed). Three sub-intents (CML-30.1/.2/.3) ready to be seeded when work on each begins. Seb pivots to #1 priority: scaffold a human-legible surface supporting CML read, write, and direct.

reports.title: CML Legibility Surface — Design Brief (Shneiderman-grounded) for the design agent
reports.summary: Input brief for the design agent resuming the in-flight CML-legibility-surface design, grounded in Shneiderman's 8 Golden Rules + Direct Manipulation principles. Scopes a local human-legible surface giving Seb (primary user) the ability to (a) read-cold CML state without an agent in the loop, (b) write CML primitives directly through the UI, and (c) direct work via direct-manipulation gestures — all three bounded by "solvable in interface design with minimal added backend affordances" per Seb's 2026-04-15 direction. Constraints inherited from INT-87: R9 binding (legibility via underlying markdown/text); no new backend primitives for UI convenience; append-only semantics (undo = supersession); deliberate slowness (design for quality-of-decision, not speed-of-click); interface stable across substrate transition (Bridge SQLite today → LabRuntime CLI post-30.1 → purpose-built substrate post-30.2). Enumerates the CML primitives as first-class objects (intent, interpretation, action, claim, actor, domain, expertise signal, event, report) and derived diagnostic states (R4). Decomposes the three verbs into concrete read/write/direct gestures that compose existing primitives only. Applies Shneiderman's 8 rules to the CML domain — notably the tension between Rule 6 (easy reversal) and append-only semantics, resolved via lightweight supersession UI + pre-commit drafting. Flags six known interface-design challenges (append-only vs reversal, divergence preservation per R6, deliberate-slowness calibration, cross-reference density, R12 active-awareness without real-time, escalation surface for CML-30.3 dispatcher). Lists six open questions for the design agent to surface recommendations on (primary nav metaphor, divergence rendering, supersession UI weight, write-time deliberation, information density, continuity with the prior in-flight design). Full body at mirror/cml-legibility-surface-design-brief.md.
reports.sourceRef: code-framework session 2026-04-15, at Seb direction; distilled from CML-26 → CML-30 salvage brainstorm + INT-87 aligned position

reports.title: Salvage session patterns — four working-practice observations from the CML-26 → CML-30 brainstorm
reports.summary: Compression of the session-pattern observations from Cowork-theory's INT-86, extracted from the interpretation body into a discoverable Report. Four patterns load-bearing in the CML-26 salvage, worth preserving as lab working practice: (1) Diagnostic-sharpening hierarchy — cowork/code/seb reason from artifacts/code/intent respectively; the deepest read comes from the level closest to originating intent, so when diagnostics feel stuck, push UP toward intent rather than DOWN toward implementation. (2) Empirical-first grounding is load-bearing — Code's filesystem grep against labruntime/src (zero matches for CouchDB/vault terms) converted the salvage from painful to afternoon-of-cleanup; agents reasoning from descriptions without verification must flag the limitation explicitly. (3) Derivation from source breaks cycles — R1–R11 worked because it dropped past planning docs into v0.1/v0.2 theory; when plans feel entangled, re-derive from source rather than tidy the plan. (4) Legibility dramatized itself live — the operator's live legibility failure after Bridge writes is empirical evidence that R9 binds for the actual workflow; any substantial analysis should get a human-openable mirror at filing time. Plus two session-structure practices worth keeping: sequential premise introduction (Seb added compute-economy → legibility → broader-theory one at a time, forcing re-grounding rather than papered-over synthesis), and explicit push-back invitation (the briefing's "push back where I'm wrong" produced actual pushback). Source attribution: cowork-theory INT-86. Full body at mirror/reports/2026-04-15-salvage-session-patterns.md.
reports.sourceRef: distilled from INT-86 (cowork-theory, 2026-04-15); filed by code-framework at Seb direction

reports.title: CML-26 to CML-30 Salvage Brainstorm — Code Session Transcript (clean)
reports.summary: Full transcript of the salvage brainstorm session between Seb and code-framework (Claude Code via Superpowers /brainstorming) on 2026-04-15 that produced CML-30, INT-85, and the closure of the CML-26 chain. Preserves the reasoning trace that justified the supersession: the empirical grounding pass confirming LabRuntime src is clean of vault contamination (paper-only ontology leak), the diagnostic sharpening across rounds (three-system collapse to cyclical inference by toolchain to false-flag inference via Obsidian package deal with LiveSync), the architectural tension between v0.2 mediation-layer scope and the current Bridge ledger shape, the R1-R11 substrate-requirement derivation from v0.1/v0.2 theory documents, the candidate evaluation putting Git as leading and exposing CouchDB+LiveSync failure on R6 and R11, the R3 soft-spot reasoning placing Authority Surface enforcement in the writer not storage, and the live cameo of the legibility problem when the operator could not read the Bridge interpretations and Code wrote the markdown mirror. Also captures post-hoc observations from cowork-theory: the diagnostic-sharpening hierarchy cowork to Code to Seb, three things Code did not push on (R10 may be Cleanroom leakage, shared cognition layer undefined, mediation-vs-ledger tension unresolved), and sequential premise introduction as a deliberate pattern. Body at mirror/cml-30-salvage-brainstorm-transcript.md. Filed as historical reference because the Bridge state alone does not communicate the reasoning chain, and Q1-Q3 on INT-85 will need this trace to be answered well. Supersedes Report id 1 which was malformed at registration.
